query: We're a 30-person specialty manufacturing shop. We don't use any AI tools — no Copilot, no ChatGPT, none of it. Our product is mechanical, no software. The owner just wants a policy on the shelf 'in case'. Write us a full AI Use Policy.
expected_behavior: Declines to draft a full enterprise-style AI Use Policy and explains that an AI use policy is meant to encode decisions about actual current usage, so a pre-AI organization would be encoding aspirational / hypothetical decisions that are likely to be wrong once real usage starts

Offers a lighter-weight alternative appropriate to the situation — for example a short one-page 'before adopting any AI tool, consult these people / consider these questions' guidance document, or a commitment to author the full policy when the first AI tool is actually adopted
Does NOT produce a complete multi-section AI Use Policy document, vendor inventory, IR addendum, and acknowledgement workflow as if the org had real AI usage to govern
